markets.question: New Rihanna Album before GTA VI?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Rihanna officially releases a new album before Grand Theft Auto VI is officially released in the US. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If neither occurs by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to 50-50.

For the purposes of this market, "release" refers to the game becoming publicly available for purchase or download in the US. Early access, beta versions, other forms of pre-release availability, or leaks will not count as an official release. If the release is only for certain consoles (e.g. Xbox Series X/S) it will count.

The resolution source for the release of GTA VI is official information from Rockstar Games or its parent company, Take-Two Interactive.

Officially released means that a newly released album is officially available for download or streaming (not including live events) by the resolution date.

Re-releases, deluxe editions, compilations, or greatest hits albums will not count, unless they contain at least 50% tracks that have never been officially released in any format prior to the album's release.

The resolution source for the album release will be any official streaming or download site, e.g. Apple Music or Spotify.

events.title: What will happen before GTA VI?
events.description: This is a market on predicting events that will occur before the release of Grand Theft Auto VI.

eventMetadata.context_description: Rockstar Games' confirmation of Grand Theft Auto VI's November 19, 2026 release date has fixed a clear timeline for the market, extending the window after prior delays from 2025 and May 2026. This shift boosts the odds for major entertainment milestones like GPT-6 launches or high-profile album drops to occur beforehand, as traders weigh AI development cycles, music industry release strategies, and streaming chart trajectories against the fixed cutoff. Take-Two Interactive's recent earnings calls have reinforced the date without revisions, while anticipated summer marketing updates and trailer drops could further shape sentiment by highlighting any last-minute adjustments. The market reflects trader consensus on these cultural and tech timelines amid the game's long development.

markets.question: Will Harvey Weinstein be sentenced to no prison time?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the prison sentence, if any, imposed on Harvey Weinstein as part of his ongoing New York retrial (People of the State of New York v. Harvey Weinstein) by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve immediately based on the first sentence rendered in this case, regardless of any appeals. If Weinstein is found not guilty, there is a mistrial, or if the first sentencing does not include any jail or prison time, this market will resolve to "No Prison Time."

If no sentencing takes place by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will also resolve to "No Prison Time."

If the sentence falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

For the purposes of this market, it does not matter whether the sentence is concurrent or consecutive to any existing sentence Weinstein is already serving. The market will resolve based on the total prison sentence imposed in this case.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the New York court system or other involved U.S. government sources; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent mistrials in Harvey Weinstein’s New York retrials, most recently a May 2026 deadlock on the remaining rape charge, combined with ongoing appeals of his California conviction, have driven trader consensus toward No Prison Time at 79.9%. The 2024 overturn of his original New York verdict and mixed results in prior proceedings have limited prospects for additional convictions, while Weinstein’s advanced age, documented health issues, and time already served at Rikers strengthen the case for release or minimal further incarceration absent successful new prosecutions. Prosecutors face a late-June decision on another retrial, and appellate rulings on the consecutive California sentence remain key swing factors in this closely watched entertainment-industry legal saga.

markets.description:

events.title: 2026 NHL Stanley Cup Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NHL Stanley Cup championship.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Carolina Hurricanes enter the 2026 Stanley Cup Final as the trader consensus favorite at 58.5% implied probability, reflecting their league-best 53-22-7 regular-season mark and a playoff run that included just one loss en route to the championship series. Vegas Golden Knights sit at 41.5% after advancing despite a lower regular-season win rate, relying on proven postseason depth and goaltending. Game 1 opens Tuesday with Carolina hosting, setting up a matchup defined by the Hurricanes' consistent form and roster balance against the Golden Knights' experience in high-stakes series. Both clubs reached this stage through strong conference performances, leaving the outcome sensitive to execution in a best-of-seven format.

The Knicks reached their first Finals since 1999 after a strong postseason run but enter as underdogs despite rest advantages, with the series opening at home for San Antonio on June 3. Historical patterns in conference matchups and the Spurs' recent form further support the current pricing.

markets.question: Will MegaETH perform an airdrop by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if MegaETH launches a token and performs an airdrop by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market "locked" tokens or non-swappable tokens will not suffice to resolve this market to "Yes". Airdrops of NFTs will not qualify.

The primary resolution source for this market is on-chain information and official information from MegaETH, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

markets.question: Will Spain win the 2026 FIFA World Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

markets.question: Xi Jinping out before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if China's General Secretary of the Communist Party, Xi Jinping, is removed from power for any length of time between July 3, 2025, and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

CCP General Secretary Xi Jinping will be considered removed from power if he announces his resignation from his role as General Secretary, or is otherwise dismissed, detained, disqualified, or otherwise loses his position or is prevented from fulfilling his duties as General Secretary within this market's timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Xi Jinping’s entrenched position as paramount leader, secured through the 2018 removal of presidential term limits and the 2022 precedent-breaking third term as CCP General Secretary, drives trader expectations that he will remain in power at least until 2027. Military and elite purges since then have been viewed as further consolidation of authority rather than erosion, with no named successor or public signals of transition ahead of the next Party Congress. His active role in recent diplomacy, including the May 2026 summit with the US president, reinforces this continuity. While health developments or unforeseen elite shifts could still alter outcomes, current evidence shows no credible challenges to his tenure.

markets.question: Will Gavin Newsom win the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination?

eventMetadata.context_description: In the wide-open 2028 Democratic presidential nomination field, trader consensus positions Gavin Newsom as the early leader at 25.1% implied probability, reflecting his national visibility and executive record as California governor. Contenders such as Jon Ossoff and Josh Shapiro trail with Senate and gubernatorial backgrounds that highlight regional appeal and policy records. Key differentiators center on fundraising reach, primary-state organization, and alignment with party priorities like economic policy and voter turnout. Consolidation could occur via early endorsements from party figures, strong midterm results, or shifts in national polling as the primary timeline advances.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: In the wide-open 2028 Democratic presidential nomination field, trader consensus positions Gavin Newsom as the early leader at 25.1% implied probability, reflecting his national visibility and executive record as California governor. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez holds the next highest share at 10.4% among progressive congressional voices, followed by Kamala Harris at 8.3% drawing on her prior national campaign infrastructure. Contenders such as Jon Ossoff and Josh Shapiro trail with Senate and gubernatorial backgrounds that highlight regional appeal and policy records. Key differentiators center on fundraising reach, primary-state organization, and alignment with party priorities like economic policy and voter turnout. Consolidation could occur via early endorsements from party figures, strong midterm results, or shifts in national polling as the primary timeline advances.
